calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: M. Armon Cooper for Keith Rutman & Seth Rutman both appearing via court call.

The Court questioned why preliminary distribution was made without court authorization. Mr. Cooper reported that the distribution was made by the administrator without consulting him, that it was done shortly after being appointed, that the assets were appraised, that the action was reported in the final report, that he (Mr. Cooper) had not sufficiently explained Mr. Rutman's role to him, that Seth Rutman is a financial advisor with UBS and that except for a minimal amount the rollovers were not actually distributed. Mr. Keith Rutman apologized to the court, stated that he would not have made the distribution if he had been aware that he could not do so, that it was a bookkeeping transaction for the IRS and that all bills have been paid. Mr. Seth Rutman apologized to the court, stated that they are all professionals, that it was an emotional time, that they were trying to get the administration behind them and that there was no ill intent. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-14-2014 Advanced From Nov-03-14 To Oct-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-14-2014 Continued To Nov-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance - M. Armon Cooper for Mr. Rutman.

Mr. Cooper stated that his petition was continued to November 3, 2014 as he failed to file the Notice of Hearing and provide an order by the deadline. He filed the documents on Friday, October 10, 2014 and asked the Court to sign his order today. He stated that the only heirs are petitioner and his two brothers. The other brothers were coming to San Francisco soon from New York and New Jersey and that he wanted Petitioner to have the Letters when they were in town in order to make the trip productive. The Court stated it would not review the documents and sign the order on the bench, but that it would advance the hearing to October 21, 2014.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Sandy Hilton.
